meta.our_scoring_note: Pulse is partly built. Bundled retainer + monthly ROI report shipped; PSG application not yet submitted; named-strategist hour bundled in Core+. AI-augmented production (Pencil-equivalent throughput) is in pilot via internal Elitez Group prompt library, not productised. Cross-language (EN + Mandarin + Bahasa) bench is real via Elitez Group ops. Scores reflect shipped-or-imminent capability, not aspiration.

strategy_canvas.headline_thesis: Pulse breaks the cluster on bundle_consolidation_depth (zero competitor collapses 8+ marketing tools/agencies into one retainer at SG SME pricing) and psg_grant_listing (no SG mid-market agency holds bundled-retainer PSG pre-approval), at the deliberate cost of holding_company_brand_craft where Ogilvy SG, Naga DDB, Edelman SG, and Mutant Communications retain the high-craft brand-campaign lane. Reframe wedge from 'geographic arbitrage' (offshore is cheap) to 'ops-stack arbitrage' (one accountable owner across 8+ tools).

dimensions.label: Bundle consolidation depth (8+ tools/agencies into one retainer)
Monthly ROI reporting cadence (CPL / CAC / payback)
Holding-company brand craft (high-end creative + earned media)

rubrics.bundle_consolidation_depth: 0 — Single line item only (logo OR ads-mgmt OR SEO). 1 — 2 line items. 2 — 3 line items. 3 — 4 line items, separate quotes. 4 — 5–6 line items in a bundled monthly retainer. 5 — 8+ line items (social + design + video + web + SEO + ads + reporting + strategy) in a single retainer with one accountable owner.
rubrics.psg_grant_listing: 0 — No SG grant posture; foreign or USD-only billing. 1 — Aware of PSG but not listed. 2 — SG entity, no grant pre-approval. 3 — Adjacent product PSG-listed (corp-sec, payroll, accounting) but not the marketing retainer. 4 — Marketing-tool-stack vendors PSG-listed (HubSpot, Mailchimp, Hootsuite, Salesforce) but not bundled-retainer. 5 — IMDA PSG pre-approved at the bundled-retainer level AND EDG-eligible AND WDG(JR+) consultant on register; single application via BGP cuts effective price ≥50%.
rubrics.transparent_published_pricing: 0 — 'Contact sales'; no figures anywhere. 1 — Pricing published but in foreign currency only with no SGD conversion. 2 — Tier names visible, prices behind a form / triangulated only. 3 — Tier prices visible in foreign currency, SGD conversion at checkout. 4 — Public per-month SGD pricing on the public site. 5 — Public SGD pricing AND post-grant effective-price calculator AND tier comparison vs. the in-house-hire baseline.
rubrics.monthly_roi_reporting_cadence: 0 — No reporting / quarterly executive deck only. 1 — Quarterly slides. 2 — Monthly slides with vanity metrics (impressions, reach). 3 — Monthly numerical report with CPL or CAC. 4 — Monthly one-page dashboard with CPL + CAC + channel mix + cohort view. 5 — Monthly board-grade dashboard with CPL + CAC + payback + attribution model + brand health, exportable to IC pre-read.
rubrics.retainer_flexibility_30d: 0 — 12-month minimum, exit penalties. 1 — 6-month minimum. 2 — 3-month minimum. 3 — 60-day notice. 4 — 30-day notice. 5 — 30-day notice + month-to-month + transparent pause / scale-down option without re-onboarding fee.
rubrics.ai_augmented_production_velocity: 0 — Manual-only production, sub-10 assets/mo. 1 — Manual + occasional template use, ~10 assets/mo. 2 — Some AI-generated assets but no integrated pipeline, ~15 assets/mo. 3 — AI-augmented production pipeline, 15–20 assets/mo. 4 — Documented AI-augmented pipeline, 20+ assets/mo across formats with QA layer. 5 — AI-augmented pipeline 30+ assets/mo with named-tool integration (Pencil / Hypotenuse / Jasper / Midjourney / Suno) AND named human-in-the-loop strategist accountability.
rubrics.regulated_vertical_compliance: 0 — Zero compliance vocabulary; generic SG agency staff. 1 — Aware of PDPA, no MOH / WSG / ECDA literacy. 2 — Handles PDPA correctly; no vertical-specific copy review. 3 — Handles PDPA + one regulated vertical (clinic OR training OR allied health) with template library. 4 — Handles PDPA + 2–3 regulated verticals with documented copy-review workflow. 5 — Handles PDPA + IM8 + MOH HSAR + WSG / IPP + ECDA with internal compliance bench, documented copy-review SLA, and PSG-approved data-handling baseline.
rubrics.holding_company_brand_craft: 0 — No brand-craft capability; templates only. 1 — Logo / template work. 2 — Junior brand-design capability, no strategy layer. 3 — Mid-market brand-design + light strategy. 4 — Award-winning craft work, integrated brand strategy, earned-media adjacency. 5 — Holding-company calibre (Ogilvy / Naga DDB / Edelman) — multi-million-dollar brand campaigns, cultural craft, journalist rolodex, festival-grade creative.

segments.name: Burnt founder of B2B services SME
segments.descriptor: 10–40 staff SG B2B services SME, hired and walked out on a SG mid-market agency 2023–25, will not pay agency fees again without monthly ROI proof.

segments.name: Scale-up in-house marketer extension
segments.descriptor: Solo marketing manager at 40–120 staff SG scale-up, drowning in production, needs a vendor that extends the role rather than replaces it.

segments.name: Regulated-vertical operator
segments.descriptor: Clinic owner / training-provider MD / allied-health practice principal under MOH HSAR / PDPA / WSG IPP rules — compliance-aware copy is mandatory.

segments.name: PE-backed growth-stage brand
segments.descriptor: 60–200 staff SG-HQ Series-A portfolio company with a 12-month board mandate to professionalise marketing ahead of a Series B raise or strategic exit.

segments.name: Gen-2 family business modernising
segments.descriptor: 20+ year-old SG family firm where younger generation is digitising marketing for the first time; patriarch signs the cheque.

segments.name: SG SME with SEA expansion ambition
segments.descriptor: SG-HQ SME 30–120 staff selling into SG + MY + ID + VN markets; needs cross-language (EN + Mandarin + Bahasa) content + multi-jurisdiction PDPA workflow.

attack_plans.niche_name: PSG-funded bundle for burnt B2B founders
attack_plans.icp: Bootstrapped or family-funded founder-CEO of a SG B2B services SME (10–40 headcount, S$1–8M revenue) who hired a SG mid-market agency in 2023–25, signed a 6-month minimum, and walked out 9 months later with quarterly decks and no leads — and is now self-funding 8+ marketing SaaS subscriptions plus 1–2 freelancers.
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: Of the S$410M SAM (55,000 SG SMEs already DIY-ing across ≥3 separate marketing tools/agencies per Agent 2), MediaPlus Digital SG Agency Survey 2025 confirms 47% described their last agency engagement as 'partly or fully a waste of money' — yielding ~25,850 burnt-founder SMEs. Conservative 65% screen-out rate to filter for the 10–40 headcount × S$1–8M revenue B2B services band leaves ~9,050 reachable SMEs. At Pulse Lite blended ARPU ~S$12,000/yr (S$799/mo Lite list price + occasional Core upgrades) per Agent 3 recommended_tiers, slice TAM = 9,050 × S$12,000 = S$108.6M. Conversion at PSG-listed effective price (S$400/mo post-grant) lands below the Mailchimp + Canva + Hootsuite combined invoice, neutralising the dominant procurement objection.
attack_plans.why_gap: Zero SG mid-market agency (Construct Digital, iFoundries, MediaOne, Verz Design, 2Stallions) currently holds IMDA PSG pre-approval at the bundled-retainer level. PSG-listed adjacent vendors (HubSpot via partner, Mailchimp via partner, Salesforce via partner, Click Academy, GoDigital Academy) ship tools or training, not the bundled-retainer + monthly ROI dashboard + 30-day-notice combination the burnt founder needs. The gap is structural: PSG pre-approval requires SG incorporation + PDPA + IM8 cybersecurity baseline + a productised SaaS-style offer the IMDA reviewer can audit — which traditional SG agencies have not invested in. 12–18 month replication window from competitor application date to IMDA listing per Agent 2's policy timeline.
attack_plans.why_we_win: Pulse our_score 5 on bundle_consolidation_depth + 4 on psg_grant_listing + 5 on transparent_published_pricing + 5 on retainer_flexibility_30d (per Agent 4 strategy canvas). PSG application targeted for 2026-Q3 submission per Agent 2 policy timeline (4–6 week IMDA approval). Bundled retainer with 30-day notice + monthly ROI dashboard is already the productised default — burnt-founder buyer recognises the format from the marketing site. PSG cuts S$799 list to S$400 effective — below the IAB SG SME impulse-buy floor (S$500–1,000/mo) for unbudgeted SaaS spend.

gtm.channel: PSG listing page on gobusiness.gov.sg + SBF Founder Pulse partnership webinar series + 'Just left your agency?' targeted LinkedIn ads to SG SME founders 10–40 headcount + warm-intro flow through Elitez Group's existing 90+ active client operations + co-founder roundtable hosted with SBF / SCCCI member firms on burnt-agency reset stories.
gtm.pitch: Replace your 8 SaaS subscriptions + 2 freelancers + DIY founder time with a PSG-funded bundled retainer that ships 20+ assets/mo, a monthly one-page ROI report, and a named senior strategist with first-name email — for less than your current Mailchimp + Canva + Hootsuite combined invoice.
gtm.pricing: Pulse Lite list S$799/mo; PSG-net effective S$400/mo. 30-day notice. No annual lock-in. Per Agent 3 recommended_tiers — single application via BGP.
gtm.content: Three assets in first 90 days: (1) ungated 'Burnt by your last agency? Here is the reset playbook' blog post comparing the typical SG founder's S$1,500/mo DIY + freelancer + ad-hoc-agency stack against the Pulse Lite bundle; (2) SBF + SCCCI co-branded 'PSG-funded marketing reset' webinar with two anonymised burnt-founder case studies; (3) PSG application step-by-step PDF for SG SME founders co-published with a SG-licensed corporate lawyer covering eligibility + filing + grant claim mechanics. Plus a 'cost of one freelancer roster vs. one Pulse retainer' calculator on the marketing site.

attack_plans.niche_name: In-house marketer extension for SG scale-ups
attack_plans.icp: Solo marketing manager / head of growth at a SG scale-up (40–120 headcount, S$5–20M revenue, post-Series-A or profitable bootstrapped) drowning in production — spends 70% of the week shipping social posts, ad creatives, blog content, and chasing the freelancer roster instead of doing the strategy / GTM work the role was hired for. The CEO / COO has approved a junior-marketer hire (S$4,500/mo all-in budget) but the recruit cycle is stalled at 12 weeks.
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: Of the ~95,000 SG SMEs in the 5–120 headcount band (DOS / SingStat SME Profile 2024), roughly ~12,000 sit in the 40–120 headcount × S$5–20M revenue intersection where a solo in-house marketer typically exists (SHRI HR Practices Survey SG 2025 cross-tab confirms ~38% of mid-market SG SMEs have a marketing function but lack production support). At Pulse Core blended ARPU ~S$24,000/yr (S$1,999/mo list + occasional Enterprise upgrades) per Agent 3, slice TAM = 12,000 × ~30% TAM-ICP overlap × S$24,000 ≈ S$86.4M. The procurement frame ('use the unfilled junior-hire budget on Pulse instead, keep your marketer senior on strategy') is the conversion lever — Robert Walters SG 2026 confirms the 12-week junior-marketer recruit cycle is the macro drag this tier exploits.
attack_plans.why_gap: BLAST + MarketerHire + Growth Collective ship fractional-CMO models (US$3–10k/mo) but offload production back to the in-house marketer; AI-content tools (Pencil, Hypotenuse, Jasper) ship velocity but not strategy + reporting + paid-media management; SG mid-market agencies (Construct Digital, iFoundries, MediaOne) sell project work that requires the in-house marketer to rebrief at every project boundary. Nobody currently sells a 'plug-in production team that the in-house marketer briefs once and never re-explains' at SG SME pricing — the gap is structural: it requires AI-augmented production velocity (4 on the rubric) PLUS a named-strategist accountability layer PLUS SG-priced retainer arithmetic (PSG-net under S$1k/mo).
attack_plans.why_we_win: Pulse our_score 5 on bundle_consolidation_depth + 5 on retainer_flexibility_30d + 4 on ai_augmented_production_velocity + 4 on monthly_roi_reporting_cadence (per Agent 4 strategy canvas). The Pulse 'extends your in-house marketer' positioning is unique — every SG mid-market competitor implicitly competes WITH the in-house marketer, not WITH the CEO's unfilled-hire budget. Elitez Group's WDG(JR+) consultant register status (LOA valid through 17 Mar 2027) layers a 70% workforce-redesign rebate for the in-house team enablement layer, dropping effective Year-1 cost on the Enterprise SKU below S$1,500/mo.

gtm.channel: LinkedIn ABM targeting 'Marketing Manager' / 'Head of Growth' titles at 40–120 staff SG SMEs (Wavemaker / Vertex / Insignia / Openspace portfolio cohort) + 'use the junior-hire budget on Pulse' content series + SHRI + AmCham SG marketing-leader webinar circuit + warm-intro through PE / VC partnership channel + co-marketing with HR consultancies serving the Series-A scale-up cohort.
gtm.pitch: You are senior. You should be doing strategy. We are the production team your CEO has already approved budget for — same monthly cost as the junior hire who is still 12 weeks from starting, ships from day one with a named senior strategist on your bench. Your in-house role becomes about channel decisions and stakeholder management; Pulse handles the briefs, the design QA, the schedule, the reporting.
gtm.pricing: Pulse Core list S$1,999/mo; PSG-net effective S$999/mo. EDG-eligible strategy / brand layer can be bundled at engagement start (~S$8–15k one-off, 50% rebate). Per Agent 3 recommended_tiers — designed to land inside the 'unfilled junior-hire budget' procurement frame.
gtm.content: Three assets in first 90 days: (1) ungated 'In-house marketer's burnout audit' diagnostic — 8 questions, 90-second scorecard, ends with the 'use the junior-hire budget on Pulse' calculator; (2) gated 'Production extension playbook' (PDF + Notion template) showing exactly which 12 production tasks Pulse takes off the in-house marketer's plate, with SLA timestamps; (3) co-hosted webinar with a SG scale-up CMO who shipped 3× content velocity in 6 months without hiring anyone. Plus an interactive ROI calculator on the marketing site comparing 'junior hire S$4,500/mo' vs. 'Pulse Core S$1,999/mo' over 12 months.

attack_plans.niche_name: Regulated-vertical SKU for SG clinics + trainers
attack_plans.icp: Clinic owner / training-provider managing director / allied-health practice principal of a SG SME (10–60 headcount, S$2–15M revenue) operating under MOH HSAR / WSG IPP / ECDA / PDPA + IM8 regulatory regimes — cannot use US-hosted AI-content tools without a vendor-side cross-border safeguard, cannot use freelancers who don't know the MOH advertising guidelines, and cannot use generic agencies that produce non-compliant 'before-after' creative.
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: Of the ~95,000 SG SMEs in the 5–120 headcount band, MOH-licensed healthcare SMEs (~3,400 across GP / dental / TCM / physio / allied-health per MOH HCSA register 2025) + WSG / IPP-listed training providers (~2,800 per WSG 2025 register) + ECDA-registered preschool groups (~1,500 per ECDA 2025) yield ~7,700 regulated-vertical SG SMEs. Of those, ~2,100 sit in the 10–60 headcount × S$2–15M revenue Pulse-priced band. At Pulse Core regulated-vertical SKU blended ARPU ~S$30,000/yr (S$2,400/mo monthly retainer + S$1,000/mo compliance-review premium) per Agent 3 recommended_tiers, slice TAM = 2,100 × S$30,000 = S$63M. The compliance-risk dominates price elasticity (Agent 3 elasticity_band: low) — buyers compare against the cost of an MOH HSAR enforcement notice (S$10–25k per 2025-Q3 case data) not against agency benchmarks.
attack_plans.why_gap: Generic SG mid-market agencies (Construct Digital, iFoundries, MediaOne, 2Stallions) lack the MOH HSAR / WSG / ECDA / IPP vocabulary their staff would need 3+ months to learn — and clinic owners get burnt during that ramp. Premium PR firms (Edelman SG, Mutant Communications) have the regulatory literacy but their pricing structurally excludes the SME budget. AI-content tools (Pencil, Hypotenuse, Jasper) cannot pass MOH HSAR copy review without human-in-the-loop layer AND most are US-hosted (PDPA + IM8 cross-border red flags for patient lists). DIY corp-sec line-by-line review at S$300/hr × 6 hr/mo = S$1,800/mo is just for compliance review — no production. The gap is structural: a vendor needs PDPA-clean SG-resident hosting + MOH-aware copy template library + WSG-aware course-outcome language + named compliance reviewer + bundled production — and nobody at SG SME pricing currently ships all five together.
attack_plans.why_we_win: Pulse our_score 4 on regulated_vertical_compliance + 5 on bundle_consolidation_depth + 5 on transparent_published_pricing + 5 on retainer_flexibility_30d (per Agent 4 strategy canvas). Elitez Group's existing regulated-vertical bench (security industry MOH-compliant comms, training-provider IPP language, PDPA + IM8 baseline already PSG-eligible track) makes the regulated-vertical SKU a 4–8 week build, not a 6-month build. WDG(JR+) eligibility for in-house compliance-team training adds a 70% rebate on the wrap engagement.

gtm.channel: Targeted outreach via SDA (Singapore Dental Association) + SMA (Singapore Medical Association) + WSG IPP partner-vendor network + ECDA preschool cluster newsletters + dedicated landing page for each regulated vertical with vertical-specific MOH / WSG / ECDA reference material + co-marketing with MOH-licensed corp-sec firms (BoardRoom, Tricor, Mazars) referring clinic owners to a compliance-aware marketing partner.
gtm.pitch: Your last MOH letter cost you a S$15k fine and 3 weeks of legal cleanup. Pulse's regulated-vertical SKU bakes MOH HSAR + PDPA + IM8 + WSG IPP literacy into every asset before it ships — your name never appears in the next enforcement bulletin. PDPA-clean SG-resident hosting by default. Named compliance reviewer on the bench. Bundled with the production team that runs your social, ads, content, and reporting.
gtm.pricing: Pulse Core regulated-vertical SKU list S$2,400/mo (S$1,999 Core + S$401 compliance-review premium); EDG-eligible compliance audit + workflow setup at engagement start (S$10–20k one-off, 50% rebate). WDG(JR+) wrap for in-house team enablement (70% rebate, S$50k cap). PSG-net effective S$1,200/mo on the recurring retainer.
gtm.content: Three assets in first 90 days: (1) ungated 'MOH HSAR self-audit checklist' for SG clinic owners — 24 questions covering before-after claims, testimonials, superlatives, comparative claims, with red-flag/green-light scoring; (2) gated 'WSG IPP course-outcome language template library' for training providers — 40 sentence templates pre-approved against IPP audit criteria; (3) co-hosted webinar with a SG-licensed healthcare lawyer + a clinic owner who passed an MOH inspection cleanly after switching to Pulse. Plus a 'cost of one MOH enforcement notice vs. one Pulse compliance retainer' calculator on the marketing site.
